title: senna cana var. phyllostegia
intro: Senna cana var. phyllostegia, also known as the 'Rio Grande Cassia', is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Senna cana var. phyllostegia lie in its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include relief from constipation, diarrhea, and skin irritations, as well as its potential use in treating fever and respiratory issues. Senna cana var. phyllostegia is commonly prepared as a dried leaf or root infusion or decoction, often consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
